entries.shortDescription: Fast-growing green macroalgae for saltwater refugia that consumes excess nitrates and phosphates; rigid tangled structure provides copepod habitat and enables easy nutrient export through harvesting.
Fast-growing green macroalgae that quickly reduces dissolved nitrate and phosphate; one of the more nutritious options and readily consumed by herbivorous fish.
Bright red and burgundy macroalgae with sharp bushy fronds; grows quickly, helps with nutrient export, and serves as healthy live food for herbivorous fish like tangs.
Hardy green macroalgae featuring fern-like fronds, valued for reef refugia and natural water filtration through nutrient absorption.
entries.notes: Shipping restrictions: not available in California or Hawaii due to invasive species regulations.

entries.shortDescription: Striking red macroalgae with sharply tapered flame-like fronds; serves dual purposes as decorative refugium addition and nutritious food source for herbivorous fish.
Vibrant green macroalgae with distinctive grape-like round leaves forming a bush-like structure; prized for nutrient export and visual appeal in reef refugia.
entries.notes: Shipping restrictions: not available in California or Hawaii due to invasive species regulations.

entries.shortDescription: Dark green macroalgae with sharp needle-like fronds; grows steadily, exports excess nutrients, and serves as live food for herbivorous fish.
Striking red macroalgae featuring deeply furcated flame-shaped fronds; works well as an ornamental in display tanks or for nutrient export in refugiums; slow-growing and tolerant of lower light.
Attractive seaweed with thick blades and blotchy pink, purple, and red coloration; functions as both refugium plant and ornamental display piece while consuming excess nutrients.
Extraordinarily attractive rhodophyte with iridescent royal blue coloration; ideal for intermediate-expert refugium keepers; prefers brighter lighting with moderate water flow.
Vibrant red macroalgae with sharp pointy fronds; fast-growing for nutrient removal and serves as live food for herbivorous fish.
entries.notes: Morphological variant of Gracilaria tikvahiae; sold as a distinct product from Green Ogo and Mocha Ogo.

entries.shortDescription: Sought-after red macroalga with deep red hues and frilly ribbon-like fronds; grows at moderate pace and works in refugiums or display tanks.
Golden-brown macroalgae variety with spike sphere morphology, distinguished by its xanthin-rich coloring; suitable for refugiums.
entries.notes: Color variant of Gracilaria tikvahiae with xanthin-rich golden-brown pigmentation.

entries.shortDescription: Fast-growing red macroalgae featuring long stringy tube-like branches; useful for nutrient export in refugiums and as nutritious food source for herbivorous fish.
Fast-growing seaweed with distinctive dark reddish-brown coloration; highly effective for nutrient reduction in refugiums and serves as nutritious live food for herbivorous fish.
entries.notes: Color variant of Gracilaria tikvahiae with dark reddish-brown pigmentation.

entries.shortDescription: Distinctive seaweed with cylindrical stubby blades; displays greenish to brownish with golden orange hues and forms dense mats ideal for shallow refugia.
True marine plant suitable for saltwater, freshwater, or brackish aquariums; functions as ornamental and slow-growing nutrient exporter in refugiums.
entries.notes: Technically a vascular plant (Rhizophoraceae), not a macroalga, but sold in AlgaeBarn's macroalgae category. Sold as 6-8" propagule.

entries.shortDescription: Exceptionally beautiful macroalgae ranging from bright red to burgundy with thick fronds; grows at moderate pace and cleans the tank by consuming excess nitrates and phosphates.
entries.notes: Species epithet not given by AlgaeBarn — listed only as 'Red Gracilaria'. Distinct from G. parvispora (Red Ogo) based on thicker frond morphology.
